title: Peer-trigger terminal durability advanced from source-binding repair to process-death recovery

claims.title: Peer runtime durability now includes stale-lock recovery after process death
claims.summary: The filesystem terminal store records lock ownership by PID, quarantines locks whose owner process is no longer alive, and tests successor recovery after SIGKILL at three persistence boundaries.
claims.falsification_or_review_route: Fetch both commits and verify the store adds owner.json-based stale-lock recovery and the test kills a worker at before-rename, after-rename-before-directory-sync, and before-lock-release.

claims.title: The earlier statement that crash-boundary testing was only a next action is no longer current
claims.summary: A prior queue update correctly left process-death crash injection as future work. Later commits implemented that work. The earlier statement remains historically valid but is superseded as a description of current implementation state.
claims.falsification_or_review_route: Inspect the prior queue update and later crash-recovery test commit. Reject supersession if the later test does not actually terminate an OS process or cover the named boundaries.

claims.title: Hosted clean-runner verification remains distinct from committed crash-recovery tests
claims.summary: The repository contains process-death recovery tests, but this continuity slice did not observe a GitHub Actions run proving those tests pass on a clean hosted runner.
claims.falsification_or_review_route: Resolve only with a retained workflow run tied to the exact tested commit or descendant, including job conclusion and test output.
